question: Tom Lee mindshare all time high by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any weekly bar in the Kaito “Mindshare over time (L12M)” chart reaches a new all-time high at any point between November 19 and 11:59 PM ET on the date specified in the title. If no new all-time high occurs, the market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source is the creator’s page on Kaito. The weekly bar chart can be viewed by clicking the creator’s name at: https://kaito.ai/mindshare-arena/ct-leaderboard

A weekly bar counts as long as its date range includes the target date. A weekly bar is considered finalized once a bar for the following week is published.

Only the weekly values shown in the L12M chart are used for resolution.

If the Kaito page is temporarily unavailable at resolution time, the market will remain open until it returns. If it becomes permanently unavailable, the market will resolve to “No.”

question: Will Ohio Revoke Any OSB License Over Event-Contract Activity by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any online sports betting provider licensed in Ohio has its license officially revoked for any period of time by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Ohio State Government and the Ohio Casino Control Commission; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

question: Ukraine agrees to limit size of armed forces before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Ukraine publicly agrees to limit the number of personnel in its armed forces by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An official pledge by Ukraine to limit the number of personnel in its armed forces will qualify for a “Yes” resolution whether as a unilateral announcement or part of an agreement with the Russian Federation.

A qualifying agreement must include a commitment by Ukraine to limit the total number of personnel in its armed forces. This includes any specific numerical cap on the number of personnel Ukraine may have in its armed forces, or any proportional limit on Ukrainian armed forces personnel (e.g., a commitment to reduce the size of the armed forces by a percentage of its current size). Limits on the number of personnel in specific branches of the Ukrainian armed forces will not count.

Other limits on Ukrainian military powers, such as the relinquishment of long-range weapons or limits on other categories of armaments or military capabilities, that do not constrain the total number of personnel in Ukraine’s armed forces, will not qualify.

An agreement by Ukraine to limit the number of personnel in its armed forces as a precondition of a more comprehensive peace process or deal will qualify, even if the agreement is not yet finalized or part of a formalized peace deal. The September 8, 1995 “Agreed Basic Principles” between Bosnia and Yugoslavia which recognized the borders and sovereignty of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and was later formalized through the Dayton Peace Agreement is an example of a qualifying agreement.

Any agreement or pledge made before the resolution date of this market will qualify, regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be an official announcement by Ukraine; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming a qualifying agreement has been made will also qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' strong consensus for "No" at 74.5% stems from Ukraine's official rejection of any military downsizing amid its defensive war against Russia's invasion, with President Zelenskyy emphasizing the need for expanded forces through recent mobilization reforms lowering the draft age to 25. Russian demands for capping Ukrainian troops near pre-2022 levels—around 100,000 personnel—persist in stalled peace talks, but Kyiv views them as existential threats and prioritizes NATO-aligned buildup. No diplomatic breakthroughs have emerged since U.S. election shifts or EU aid pledges, and with combat intensifying in Donbas, the odds reflect skepticism of any agreement before 2027 absent a ceasefire.

question: Will Aristotle self-certify sports event contracts by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed Designated Contract Market (DCM) self-certifies sports-related event-based contracts with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) at any point before March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information released by the CFTC or the respective DCM; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus leans toward limited adoption, with Kalshi—already self-certifying and launching NFL, NBA, and MLB event contracts in late 2024 following CFTC approval—dominant at high implied probabilities, while others like CME and CBOE trail due to historical caution on gaming-related products. Key drivers include the post-Kalshi lawsuit regulatory thaw distinguishing sports from elections, yet persistent CFTC scrutiny under 17 CFR 40.11 on prohibited "gaming" contracts. Recent Kalshi volume surges signal viability amid the $150B U.S. sports betting boom, but no rival announcements heighten skepticism for multiples by March 31, 2026 resolution. Upcoming CFTC reviews or peer filings could shift odds toward broader participation.

question: Will the maximum Arctic sea ice extent this winter be <14m square kilometers?
description:
This market will resolve according to the maximum Arctic sea ice extent for all days between November 20, 2025 and April 1, 2026, as published by the National Snow and Ice Data Center.

This market will remain open until data has been published for April 1, 2026, at which point it will resolve immediately. Any revisions to sea ice extent recorded after data is published for April 1, 2026 will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market measures Arctic sea ice extent to thousands of square kilometers (e.g. 14.255 million sq km). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the National Snow and Ice Data Center, specifically the maximum value recorded for any day between November 20, 2025 and April 1, 2026 in the “NH-Daily-Extent” tab of the “Sea Ice Index Daily Extent” data set, available at https://nsidc.org/sea-ice-today/sea-ice-tools. If this resolution source becomes unavailable, another resolution source will be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly backs a maximum Arctic sea ice extent of 14.2-14.4 million square kilometers this winter (93% implied probability), mirroring the 2024 peak of 14.33 million sq km—the 19th lowest in the 46-year NSIDC satellite record—and recent years' subdued levels around 14.5-14.8 million sq km. NSIDC data as of mid-December 2024 shows extent at about 12.6 million sq km, tracking below the 1981-2010 average but akin to 2023-2024's trajectory amid persistent warm anomalies over the Barents and Bering Seas slowing refreezing. This positioning reflects the long-term decline driven by Arctic amplification. A challenge would require rare, sustained cold outbreaks or blocked ice export, boosting growth beyond precedents, though models from NOAA and ECMWF forecast continuation of low extents.

question: Will Micah Lasher be the democratic nominee for NY-12?
description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the NY-12 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on June 23, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Micah Lasher at 50.5% implied probability to win the NY-12 Democratic primary, driven by his robust fundraising exceeding $1 million, key endorsements from Manhattan Democratic clubs, and experience as a former state official targeting tech policy issues. Markets reflect skin-in-the-game assessments amid fluid field dynamics.

question: New COVID variant of concern before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the CDC identifies a new COVID variant of concern between December 1, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

The CDC's variant classification scheme can be found here: https://stacks.cdc.gov/view/cdc/107682.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the CDC.

events.title: New COVID variant of concern before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the CDC identifies a new COVID variant of concern between December 1, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

The CDC's variant classification scheme can be found here: https://stacks.cdc.gov/view/cdc/107682.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the CDC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders overwhelmingly back "No" at 87.5% implied probability for a new COVID variant of concern before 2027, anchored by the absence of any WHO-designated VOC since Omicron emerged in late 2021. This consensus reflects high global immunity from vaccines and prior infections, which has curbed severe outcomes even from recent strains like XEC and KP.3.1.1—currently classified only as variants under monitoring despite modest immune evasion. Recent CDC and WHO data show declining hospitalizations and no alarming transmission spikes, reinforcing stability. While surprises like zoonotic jumps or waning vaccine protection could shift odds, sustained surveillance tempers such risks through 2026.

question: FIFA World Cup 2026 Qualification Longshots Parlay
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if all Italy, Bolivia, and DR Congo qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for one of these teams to qualify for the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they cannot reach the required number of points to advance from their group or qualify for playoffs), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or the qualifying stage has not been completed by September 30, 2026, 11:59 PM, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be FIFA.  (https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup).

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Qualification Longshots Parlay
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if all Italy, Bolivia, and DR Congo qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for one of these teams to qualify for the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they cannot reach the required number of points to advance from their group or qualify for playoffs), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or the qualifying stage has not been completed by September 30, 2026, 11:59 PM, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be FIFA.  (https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup).

question: Tush Push banned for 2026 NFL Season?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the NFL officially announces a rule change that prohibits, limits, or penalizes the “tush push” from being used in the 2026 season before the start of the first regular season game of the 2026 NFL season. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

The “tush push” refers to a football play in which an offensive player or multiple offensive players line up directly behind the quarterback and push the quarterback forward immediately after the snap. A qualifying rule change must affect the use of this play. Partial bans, such as rules that ban pushing the quarterback only in certain situations (e.g. on quarterback sneaks), restrict who can push the quarterback, or impose penalties that specifically target the “tush push” formation or execution, will qualify.

The market will resolve based on the first official announcement from the NFL. If the league announces that the tush push will not be banned for the 2026 NFL season, this market will resolve to “No”.

Announcements of future rule changes affecting the use of the "tush push" which don't apply to the 2026 NFL season will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be official NFL announcements however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from Florida.

If no 2026 Florida Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Florida Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ashley Moody's dominant 91% implied probability in the Florida Republican Senate primary reflects her entrenched position as the state's popular Attorney General, bolstered by superior fundraising exceeding $10 million, endorsements from Governor Ron DeSantis and top GOP donors, and consistent double-digit leads in recent polls like the August RMG Research survey showing her at 65% support. Trader consensus prices in her prosecutorial record on issues like border security and election integrity, appealing to the base amid low visibility for challengers like Hamilton, Lang, and Toulme. Realistic challenges include a late-surge endorsement from Donald Trump, a Moody scandal surfacing before the August 20 primary, or energized turnout from niche activist groups, though historical Florida GOP primaries favor established incumbents.

question: Will Cole Bettles receive the most votes in the CA-11 primary?
description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who receives the most votes in the primary, regardless of party, to contest the seat for California's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The California primary is scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.sos.ca.gov/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scott Wiener leads trader consensus at 63% implied probability to win the CA-11 primary for Nancy Pelosi's open seat, driven by his strong fundraising—over $1 million raised early—incumbent state senate experience, and endorsements from local Democratic leaders like San Francisco Mayor London Breed. Saikat Chakrabarti's 21.2% reflects his national progressive profile as a Justice Democrats co-founder and AOC aide, bolstered by recent Justice Dems support amid a crowded field. Connie Chan's 12.6% stems from her San Francisco supervisor role and Asian American community ties. Recent polls, like a December Echelon Insights survey showing Wiener at 28%, reinforce this, though the March 2026 top-two primary leaves room for shifts from upcoming endorsements and debates.

question: Another Canada election called by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the next Canadian federal election is officially scheduled for a date prior to October 2029, by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For this market to resolve to "Yes" it is only necessary that the election date be declared, not that the election actually occur within the market timeframe. (e.g. if on December 23, 2025 an election is scheduled for November 23, 2026, this market will resolve to "Yes").

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the Canada, However a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Another Canada election called by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the next Canadian federal election is officially scheduled for a date prior to October 2029, by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For this market to resolve to "Yes" it is only necessary that the election date be declared, not that the election actually occur within the market timeframe. (e.g. if on December 23, 2025 an election is scheduled for November 23, 2026, this market will resolve to "Yes").

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the Canada, However a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' strong consensus against a Canadian federal election being called by June 30—implied at 92.5% for "No"—stems from the stability of Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's minority Liberal government, buttressed by the NDP's confidence-and-supply agreement extending into 2025. The federal budget passed in mid-April 2024 with NDP support quelled immediate non-confidence risks raised by NDP Leader Jagmeet Singh earlier that month. Despite Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre's double-digit polling lead (around 40% vs. Liberals' 22-25% in recent Nanos and Abacus surveys), no dissolution request to the Governor General has surfaced, and fixed-date election laws favor October 2025 amid logistical hurdles for a snap vote. Upcoming byelections in June may test dynamics, but current parliamentary calm underpins low snap election odds.

question: Will Israel annex any territory by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel officially annexes any territory between July 22, 2025 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Annexation is defined as an official declaration or legal act by the Israeli government claiming sovereignty over territory they were not claiming at the time of this market's creation.

Qualifying examples of annexation include the 1980 Jerusalem Law, and the Golan Heights Law, however instances where Israeli settlers claiming administrative control over land without a formal annexation will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Israeli government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will Israel annex any territory by December 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel officially annexes any territory between July 22 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Annexation is defined as an official declaration or legal act by the Israeli government claiming sovereignty over territory they were not claiming at the time of this market's creation.

Qualifying examples of annexation include the 1980 Jerusalem Law, and the Golan Heights Law, however instances where Israeli settlers claiming administrative control over land without a formal annexation will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Israeli government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probabilities for Israel formally annexing territory by December 31, amid the ongoing Gaza war and lack of official Knesset action. Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich continues advocating West Bank Area C sovereignty extension, but Prime Minister Netanyahu prioritizes military operations over legislative moves, facing UN and Biden administration opposition. Recent escalations include IDF seizures in the West Bank, yet these fall short of annexation. The November 5 US election looms large—a Trump win could ease US restraints, per his past support, while ceasefire talks or international rulings may further delay. Uncertainty persists given diplomatic and legal barriers.

question: Will China invade Taiwan by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of the Republic of China (Taiwan) by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Territory under the administration of the Republic of China, including any inhabited islands, will qualify; however, uninhabited islands will not qualify. 

The resolution source for this market will be official confirmation by China, Taiwan, the United Nations, or any permanent member of the UN Security Council; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Will China invade Taiwan by March 31, 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of the Republic of China (Taiwan) by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Territory under the administration of the Republic of China, including any inhabited islands, will qualify; however, uninhabited islands will not qualify. 

The resolution source for this market will be official confirmation by China, Taiwan, the United Nations, or any permanent member of the UN Security Council; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' near-certain consensus against a Chinese invasion of Taiwan by March 31, 2026, reflects the immense military hurdles of a cross-strait amphibious assault, including PLA Navy limitations against Taiwan's defenses and likely US intervention via the Taiwan Relations Act. Recent routine air and sea drills post-Taiwan's January election signal coercion, not mobilization, amid Beijing's economic slowdown and focus on internal stability over high-risk escalation. No verified preparations for invasion-scale operations exist, per defense analyses. Realistic catalysts for repricing include a provocative Taiwan independence move or post-US election deterrence erosion, though current postures and historical restraint make these unlikely.

question: Will The Clearing Company self-certify sports event contracts by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed Designated Contract Market (DCM) self-certifies sports-related event-based contracts with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) at any point before March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information released by the CFTC or the respective DCM; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Which DCMs self-certify sports event contracts by March 31, 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed Designated Contract Market (DCM) self-certifies publicly tradable sports-related event-based contracts with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) at any point before March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information released by the CFTC or the respective DCM; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus leans toward limited adoption, with Kalshi—already self-certifying and launching NFL, NBA, and MLB event contracts in late 2024 following CFTC approval—dominant at high implied probabilities, while others like CME and CBOE trail due to historical caution on gaming-related products. Key drivers include the post-Kalshi lawsuit regulatory thaw distinguishing sports from elections, yet persistent CFTC scrutiny under 17 CFR 40.11 on prohibited "gaming" contracts. Recent Kalshi volume surges signal viability amid the $150B U.S. sports betting boom, but no rival announcements heighten skepticism for multiples by March 31, 2026 resolution. Upcoming CFTC reviews or peer filings could shift odds toward broader participation.
